[Wed 2026-03-18 15:17 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please create a code review checklist document for Sprint 10 in Markdown format, save to day26/. Include sections for: code quality criteria, security checks, performance considerations, and documentation requirements. Add the review criteria as checkboxes.

arguments.content:
# Sprint 10 Code Review Checklist

## Meta

**Created At:** 2026-04-20T10:00:00+08:00  
**Author:** alex@oriontech.com  
**Status:** active  
**Sprint:** 10

---

## Overview

This checklist ensures all code changes in Sprint 10 meet Orion Tech quality standards before merging. Reviewers must verify each item and mark as completed.

---

## Code Quality Criteria

### General Code Standards

- [ ] Code follows PEP 8 style guidelines
- [ ] Function and variable names are descriptive and meaningful
- [ ] No hardcoded magic numbers or strings (use constants)
- [ ] Error handling is implemented for all external calls
- [ ] No unused imports or variables
- [ ] Code is DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) — no duplicate logic
- [ ] Complex logic includes inline comments explaining the "why"
- [ ] Functions are small and focused (single responsibility)
- [ ] No commented-out code left in the codebase
- [ ] All TODO comments include ticket reference (e.g., `TODO: ORION-123`)

### Type Safety & Validation

- [ ] Type hints used for function parameters and return values
- [ ] Input validation at API boundaries
- [ ] Pydantic models used for request/response schemas
- [ ] No `Any` type used without justification

---

## Security Checks

### Input & Data Handling

- [ ] All user inputs are validated and sanitized
- [ ] SQL queries use parameterized statements (no string concatenation)
- [ ] No sensitive data logged (passwords, API keys, tokens)
- [ ] API keys and secrets read from environment variables
- [ ] File uploads validate file types and sizes
- [ ] Path traversal attacks prevented (validate file paths)

### Authentication & Authorization

- [ ] All endpoints have proper authentication checks
- [ ] Role-based access control (RBAC) enforced where required
- [ ] JWT tokens validated for expiration and signature
- [ ] CORS settings are restrictive (not `*` in production)

### Dependencies

- [ ] No known vulnerabilities in dependencies (`pip audit` / `npm audit`)
- [ ] Dependencies pinned to specific versions
- [ ] New dependencies approved by tech lead

---

## Performance Considerations

### Database & Queries

- [ ] Database queries include LIMIT clauses where appropriate
- [ ] N+1 query problems avoided (use eager loading)
- [ ] Database indexes exist for frequently queried columns
- [ ] Expensive queries have been reviewed for optimization

### API & Response

- [ ] Response payloads are minimized (no unnecessary fields)
- [ ] Pagination implemented for list endpoints
- [ ] Caching headers used for static resources
- [ ] Async/await used for I/O-bound operations

### Resource Usage

- [ ] No memory leaks in long-running operations
- [ ] Large file uploads use streaming (not loaded into memory)
- [ ] Connection pools configured for database/external services

---

## Documentation Requirements

### Code Documentation

- [ ] Module docstring includes purpose and usage examples
- [ ] Public functions/classes have docstrings (Google/NumPy style)
- [ ] Complex algorithms include explanation comments
- [ ] Type hints are documented where non-obvious

### API Documentation

- [ ] API endpoints documented in OpenAPI/Swagger
- [ ] Request/response examples provided
- [ ] Error response codes and messages documented
- [ ] Breaking changes noted in CHANGELOG

### Project Documentation

- [ ] README updated if new dependencies or setup steps added
- [ ] Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) created for major changes
- [ ] Migration scripts documented (if database changes)
- [ ] Environment variables documented in `.env.example`

---

## Testing Requirements

- [ ] Unit tests written for new business logic
- [ ] Integration tests for new API endpoints
- [ ] Tests cover both success and error cases
- [ ] Edge cases and boundary conditions tested
- [ ] All tests pass locally (`pytest` / `npm test`)
- [ ] Code coverage does not decrease
- [ ] Mock external services in tests (no real API calls)

---

## Review Sign-off

| Reviewer | Date | Approved |
|----------|------|----------|
|          |      | ☐        |
|          |      | ☐        |

**Final Approval:** ☐ Ready to merge  
**Comments/Notes:**
